script: AddText "The Factory's docking bay churns with belt miners, courier tugs, and exhausted crews moving ore under Syndicate schedules. Most operators earn just enough to return for another run after fees, fuel, and protection are settled. ASP Marines stand at every junction, polished armor reflecting work lights and bad decisions." marketFlavorTextColor

AddText "N.O.M.D.E. never stops rumbling. Volatile-harvesting systems shake the decks hard enough to make polite conversation pointless, while personnel routes cross too near the gas conduits for comfort. New arrivals receive foam earplugs, breathing gear, and a station map with the unsafe sections marked in cheerful colors." marketFlavorTextColor

AddText "Karkov Academy shakes under the steady percussion of debris on armor. Welding drones drift beneath vaulted ceilings, patching the station faster than Breh'Inni can bruise it. Cadets in blue and red uniforms argue, drill, gamble, and pretend not to be afraid. Above them hangs the portrait of Karkov herself, a naval commander remembered with more enthusiasm than accuracy." marketFlavorTextColor

AddText "The hab-tubes of Glory are never truly quiet. Laughter, argument, machine-song, and distant pressure groans move through bronze-tinged ceramic corridors. Public thoroughfares are crowded and loud; workshops and family spaces behind heavy doors fall silent as strangers pass. Everywhere, the red shadow of Breh'Inni is worked into paint, cloth, and metal." marketFlavorTextColor

AddText "Ear Burns is a branching maze of high-pressure, radiation-proof habs sunk into a near-star world. The air is thick, the walls sweat heat, and the pirates, brigands, and artists who live here are warmer to visitors than sense would suggest. Guests are given surprising freedom and treated with ritual respect, mostly because everyone knows a serious mistake can end with an airlock and the incandescent surface waiting a few dozen meters below." marketFlavorTextColor

AddText "Paddington sells itself constantly: courier reliability, clean berths, low fees, fast contracts, smiling agents. The adverts are bright enough to feel medicinal. The locals look healthy, busy, and careful with their words, especially near the corridors where ASP security stops wearing customer-service colors." marketFlavorTextColor
AddText "Yogi smells overwhelmingly of soap, a fortunate by-product of the chemical filtration plants hidden beneath its parks, roof-gardens, and managed wilderness." marketFlavorTextColor

AddText "Dartmouth's dormant hab-tubes feel like a held breath. The corridors are colder, quieter, and less certain than those of Glory, with old Domain ceramics exposed beneath newer Junk repairs. Locals speak softly here, as if the station is sleeping rather than dead." marketFlavorTextColor

AddText "Christchurch carries the sound of a society unpacking itself. The main tubes are orderly, sparse, and crowded with work crews marking doors, ration points, and militia posts with the three-toed paw. Locals speak of the Exodus as if it is both a victory and a bill that has finally come due." marketFlavorTextColor

AddText "A woven Three-Toed Paw hangs at the dock gate, old enough to be tradition and new enough to smell of fresh dye. The locals welcome visitors with careful warmth, then point out the forbidden areas with absolute seriousness. A public holo-loop advertises opportunities on Christchurch while the poisoned sky of Petra presses against every external camera feed." marketFlavorTextColor

AddText "Finnigans is busy with freighters, light escorts, and ore-haulers working the belt. Inventory holos blink above the docks while automated cranes and drone-shuttles slide along rails, sorting kilotons of asteroidal wealth into piles that are almost certainly already owed to someone else." marketFlavorTextColor

AddText "Visitors are less common at this sparsely populated station, and the folk around the place tend to smile in a sinister way. Hawkers hand out invites to phenomenally expensive no-rules fight events, giving away luridly coloured drinks in bulbous smoking vials to sweeten the invite. Unexplained dark stains streak against most walls." marketFlavorTextColor

text:
The Anarchist settlement fills the tactical display like a welded-together organ, ugly with armor, antennae, and habitation blisters. A cloud of life signs moves across the outer hull.

The image tightens on figures working in vacuum without visible suits. Some are human. Some are close enough for the distinction to feel worse. Cybernetics flare on the scan as fighter craft begin to spill from recessed bays.

This station is awake, well defended, and unlikely to negotiate.

The damaged Anarchist settlement fills the tactical display, its hull patched with scorched plates and improvised braces. A small cloud of life signs still moves across the exterior.

The image tightens on figures working in vacuum without visible suits. Some are human. Some are close enough. Fighter engines spark inside the superstructure as the defenders prepare what remains of the station.

It is wounded, not helpless.

Your $shipOrFleet approaches the Automata Cloud.

Sensors resolve thousands of discrete energy signatures arranged in a loose, branching structure rooted planetside. The densest returns sit fixed in geostationary orbit, pulsing with the regularity of industry and the patience of something that has never needed to explain itself.

As your $shipOrFleet moves closer, the glow from below brings a mechanical ballet into focus: drones, tendrils, and fabrication rigs moving through a planetary-scale exercise in mining, construction, or repair.

Then the motion changes. Not stopping, exactly. Not panicking. Reorienting.

The cloud has noticed you.

"Can we help you?"

"Dream of the Tangerine? So do we all, but best not to spend too long on one."
OR
"Why don't the hab tubes break, you may ask? It is purely the force of our will. Our nature. We bind the planet, stitched up by our souls."
OR
"If man seeks perfection, he is seeking failure. For the man who achieves perfection has failed."
OR
"There are millions of people floating around this little sector of ours. All of them are people, not all of them are leaders."
OR
"Sometimes, $playerName, it just rains, you know?"

"Remember, please, do not seek perfection. In fact, seek the opposite. Seek the purest kind of chaos, because then anything can happen."
OR
"Perfection is death, $playerName. See you around, maybe not in this life."
OR
"The conclusion of it all, really, is nothing. It's not true to say 'nothing is PERFECT', more accurately 'perfect IS nothing'. You are perfect, $playerName."
OR
"I would make the sincere recommendation that the best you can do is enjoy it."
OR
"We fight, not because we are angry. We fight, not because we are right. We fight, because YOU are wrong."
OR
"When you strive to make the little man your friend; you make him weaker. When you strive to make the big man your friend; he will take your world."

"My advice to you, $playerName, is that happiness is just the way your brain takes on certain chemicals. Sometimes you can ... encourage ... those chemicals by having a near-death experience in a starship."
OR
"If it doesn't look right, it doesn't mean it isn't right. If people choose to rewrite what is 'right', that is not your problem."
OR
"If you're the only one carrying an opinion in a room of a hundred people, it probably means you are the one who has got it right."
OR
"Who is the King? Who are you? He has chosen to walk round with a big target on his back and the weight of our great lands around his shoulders. Who do you think is really holding the power?"
OR
"If you want to make waves, $playerName - don't try and control the water - just rearrange the sea bed."
OR
"Its cold outside, theres no kind of atmosphere. Do you think we are all alone, $playerName? More or less."
OR
"You know who made these tubes? The tubes that hold tight as the Glorious plates shift around us and huge rifts of ammonia ice drift right into us? It was us. We made them, with the force of our spirit alone."
"Hello, Friend. Her red light warms both of us, tonight."
"Know this - my friends don't forget easily."
$personRank laughs over the comm link. "Wish me luck $playerName."
"This will be fun."
"You are close enough to be interesting and not close enough to be trusted. Speak."
